text: There is no significant difference in VO2max improvement between polarized and pyramidal training intensity distributions in endurance athletes overall.
There is no significant difference in time-trial performance between polarized and pyramidal training intensity distributions in endurance athletes overall.
Polarized training produced no statistically significant differences in VO2max or time-trial performance compared to any other training intensity distribution model.
Competitive athletes may have greater VO2max improvements with polarized training compared to pyramidal training, while recreational athletes may improve more with pyramidal training.
The adaptations to VO2max following different training intensity distributions are dependent on the athlete's performance level.
Recreational endurance athletes may benefit more from a pyramidal training intensity distribution than a polarized one for VO2max improvement.
population: recreational endurance athletes (VO2max ~46.6-68.3 mL/kg/min range across studies)
